architecture.summary: KG-RAG is a three-stage pipeline for knowledge-intensive question answering over Large Language Model Agents (LMAs). Stage 1 (Storage): unstructured text is converted to Knowledge Graph triples via few-shot LLM prompting, with support for nested triple hypernodes to capture complex relational contexts; embeddings are computed and stored in a vector database. Stage 2 (Retrieval): Chain of Explorations (CoE) algorithm sequentially traverses the KG by combining vector similarity search, Cypher queries, and LLM-guided filtering to locate relevant multi-hop paths. Stage 3 (Answer Generation): the LLM synthesizes retrieved KG paths into a final answer via standard RAG prompting. Information flows from text → triples → KG database → CoE exploration → LLM synthesis.
trained_components: CoE planning and evaluation prompts (few-shot LLM)

biblio.title: KG-RAG: Bridging the Gap Between Knowledge and Creativity

claims.statement: KG-RAG achieved a hallucination rate of 15%, significantly lower than the 30% observed in Embedding-RAG.
KG-RAG achieved an Exact Match (EM) of 19% and an F1 Score of 25%.
KG-RAG demonstrated an accuracy of 32%, compared to 46% for Embedding-RAG.

KG-RAG integrates structured Knowledge Graphs with LLMs to significantly reduce the reliance on the latent knowledge of LLMs, thereby reducing hallucinated content and addressing information hallucinations, catastrophic forgetting, and limitations in processing long contexts.

Chain of Explorations (CoE) performs step-by-step exploration through KG nodes and relationships using a combination of vector similarity search, Cypher queries, and LLM-guided filtering and evaluation to retrieve relevant multi-hop paths.
The average CoE took between 4 and 5 steps over the KG until reaching the answer nodes.

KG-RAG results are lower compared to top performers on the CWQ leaderboard (human EM 63%, best machine EM 33.2%) and lower than Embedding-RAG, indicating potential areas for improvement in KG-RAG's ability to accurately parse and retrieve relevant information.

High costs associated with converting web snippets to KG triples using LLMs limited the ability to use all web snippets and perform tests on the entire development split of the CWQ dataset; performance of the LLM used for KG construction occasionally generated incorrect triples or missed triple extractions, propagating errors through CoE.
